proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 113
; PROVEN: [...fnContracts].sort(...) is a length-preserving permutation

(declare-const fn_contract_count Int)
(declare-const sorted_count Int)

; Precondition: only groups with >= 2 reach this block
(assert (>= fn_contract_count 2))

; Transition: spread copies every element; sort rearranges in place
(assert (= sorted_count fn_contract_count))

; Negation of property
(assert (not (= sorted_count fn_contract_count)))

(check-sat)

proven.claim: PROVEN: For all 0 <= i < sorted.length-1, sorted[i].line <= sorted[i+1].line
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 113
; PROVEN: For all 0 <= i < sorted.length-1, sorted[i].line <= sorted[i+1].line

(declare-const line_i Int)
(declare-const line_j Int)

; Precondition: valid source-code line numbers
(assert (> line_i 0))
(assert (> line_j 0))

; Transition: comparator = (a,b) => a.line - b.line → ascending sort
(assert (<= line_i line_j))

; Negation of property: some adjacent pair violates order
(assert (> line_i line_j))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Two contracts at the same line produce a P2 check with no temporal gap
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 113
; VIOLATION: Two contracts at the same line produce a P2 check with no temporal gap

(declare-const line_a Int)
(declare-const line_b Int)
(declare-const earlier_line Int)
(declare-const later_line Int)
(declare-const temporal_gap Int)   ; = later_line - earlier_line; must be > 0 for P2 to be meaningful

; Transition: both contracts are in the same function group
(assert (= line_a line_b))

; Transition: sort comparator returns 0 for equal lines → either ordering is valid
(assert (= earlier_line line_a))
(assert (= later_line line_b))

; Transition: temporal_gap computed from assigned ordering
(assert (= temporal_gap (- later_line earlier_line)))

; Violation: gap is zero → no mutation can have occurred between the two "points"
(assert (= temporal_gap 0))

(check-sat)
